item.title: World Cup 2026: Cowboy country turns into football epicentre in Dallas, Texas
item.description: Japan and Netherlands fans are turning out in big numbers in Dallas, Texas, as the city embraces its status as the main World Cup hub.

item.title: AI company Firmus to become Tasmania's biggest power user if three planned sites come to fruition
item.description: Artificial intelligence company Firmus Technologies will become Tasmania's biggest power user if its ambitions to establish three "AI factories" in the state come to fruition.

item.title: Call for public library funding reform as Melbourne's growth areas miss out
item.description: Local councils and Public Libraries Victoria say the way public libraries are funded across Victoria needs reform, with communities in Melbourne's growth corridors described as "chronically underfunded".

item.title: Swiss voters set to reject population cap in referendum
item.description: A projection shows voters prioritising economic stability and the country's ties with the European Union over immigration concerns.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroo Nestory Irankunda knew how he would celebrate before even taking the field
item.description: Nestory Irankunda's childhood hero is none other than Tim Cahill. So it was fitting that more than 20 years after Cahill's famous celebration, this generation's Socceroos hero paid tribute.

item.title: Concerns over Great Artesian Basin water impacts in new BHP agreement
item.description: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

item.title: World Cup: Pat Beach's Socceroos dream is Mat Ryan's nightmare, and Mark Schwarzer can relate to both
item.description: Pat Beach became a surprise national hero with his performance against Türkiye, while incumbent goalkeeper Mat Ryan watched from the bench. Mark Schwarzer has experienced both sides of that coin.

item.title: PFAS queries continue as Wagga residents reassured drinking water is safe
item.description: Wagga Wagga has two known sites contaminated by "forever chemicals" and some say not enough information about how it is being managed is available.

item.title: Gold Coast nurse charged over alleged offences involving six teenage girls
item.description: Detectives charged the 58-year-old man following investigations into the alleged indecent treatment of children between November 2020 and May 2024.

item.title: Cooper Connolly hits stunning century to hand Australia victory over Bangladesh in third ODI
item.description: Cooper Connolly fights off his body going into shock due to cramps to record his maiden international century.

item.title: Northern Grampians Shire Council ignores community calls for CCTV at Stawell crime 'hotspot'
item.description: Parents are working to petition a west Victorian council to install CCTV cameras at a skate park that police are calling a crime "hotspot". The council says taking on a public safety role is "unreasonable".

item.title: 'Potential explosive hazards' at Shackleton and Scott's historically important huts in Antarctica
item.description: Chemicals found inside two of Antarctica's most important historical sites, Earnest Shackleton's and Robert Falcon Scott's huts, could pose "potential explosive hazards", a report has warns.

item.title: A health worker holding an inject that cures syphilis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos hero Nestory Irankunda hits back as Türkiye star says his team 'dominated'
item.description: Socceroos goalscoring hero Nestory Irankunda says Türkiye could not back up its talk, despite one of its stars claiming post-game Australia had been "dominated".

item.title: Alex de Minaur claims runner-up in Rosmalen Championships after tie-break loss in final
item.description: Australia's top-ranked men's player Alex de Minaur fights to a tie-break in the Rosmalen Championships final but falls to his Polish opponent.

item.title: Australians are dying from syphilis, a disease that was almost eliminated more than a decade ago
item.description: Forty-two babies in Australia died from congenital syphilis between 2011 and 2025, with the outbreak driving health workers into communities to raise awareness of the old-world disease.

item.title: WA needle exchanges raise concerns over 'dramatic' rise in injectable peptide use
item.description: Needle exchanges in Western Australia are reporting dramatic increases in people accessing their services for peptide use.

item.title: A lost Sam Pang went looking for something. He found comedy
item.description: This workplace satire starring Sam Pang has Tasmania's first ever AFL team firmly in its sights.

item.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale
item.description: A Kimberley iron ore mine with idyllic views across a tropical sea will close after 70 years of operation as a new phase begins for offshore oil and gas.

item.title: Musician and online personality Oliver Tree presumed dead after helicopter crash
item.description: Ahead of his Australian tour, the American musician and online personality is reportedly one of six passengers involved in a fatal helicopter collision.

item.title: World Cup: FIFA's hydration break farce the ultimate insult for weary football fans
item.description: Football fans are forced to endure much to keep their love of the beautiful game alive, but FIFA's cynical new "hydration breaks" are stretching the friendship like never before.

item.title: Bureau of Meteorology's only SA wave buoy offline for third time in two years
item.description: The wave buoy off Kangaroo Island is the only such device operated by the Bureau of Meteorology in South Australia and is relied upon by professional divers and fishers.

item.title: Magnitude-2.9 earthquake felt near large dam in NSW Snowy Mountains
item.description: The magnitude-2.9 earthquake was felt in parts of the Snowy Mountains just before 1pm on Sunday, according to Geosceince Australia.

item.title: Tasmanian man allegedly caught drink driving three times in just over a week
item.description: A Tasmanian man has been charged after being allegedly caught drink driving for the third time in just over a week, with him having to be cut from his car in one instance.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Curaçao's special goal and De Oranje brighten up day four
item.description: De Oranje hit the World Cup and in the middle of a 7-1 thumping, Curaçao's lone goal might have meant the most of all. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Former police officer steps aside from country football over sexual assault charges
item.description: A female footballer has "stepped aside" from her country Victoria club after sexual assault allegations against her were revealed.

item.title: Why Cameron Munster is always Queensland's State of Origin saviour
item.description: Ever since his first State of Origin match, Queensland has looked to Cameron Munster in times of trouble. Now, with the series on the line at the MCG, his state will ask its greatest modern hero to do so again.

item.title: Lebanon and nuclear weapons: The stumbling blocks in Trump's Iran negotiation
item.description: The US-Iran deal faces five volatile days as questions remain over nuclear weapons and Israel's attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: 'Fakeaway' recipes to feed the family
item.description: Ordering in of an evening can become a costly exercise but these five tasty recipes are sure to hit the spot.

item.title: Proposal to rename Homebush dumped after councillor's conflict of interest snag
item.description: A contentious proposal to rename most of Homebush to Strathfield drew criticism as an attempt to lift property values.

item.title: South Australian community, family reacts to Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal
item.description: The family and community of Socceroos star Nestory Irankunda are "very emotional" after his goal-scoring World Cup debut. His cousin says he will be "forever grateful" for the support the family received after arriving in Australia, and "their blessing is seeing this at the big stage".

item.title: Victoria Police investigate off-field brawl during Ararat and Lismore-Derrinallum football match
item.description: Victoria Police is investigating an off-field fight at a local football match in Ararat over the weekend, sparking concerns about "dreadful" behaviour at grassroots sporting events.

item.title: Former AFL footballer Nick Stevens jailed over $170k Mildura pool fraud
item.description: An ex-footballer has been locked up after a jury found him guilty of defrauding multiple families with his regional pool business.

item.title: NT government introduces new air quality testing plan, including measuring carcinogenic emissions
item.description: The Northern Territory government has announced a plan to introduce new air quality monitoring of toxic pollutants, including any carcinogenic emissions. It follows a review that found gas giant Inpex had underestimated its emissions in Darwin.

item.title: Fuel excise rebate call next week as Anthony Albanese says ceasefire does not mean 'return to normal'
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will give Australians "appropriate notice" about the fuel excise rebate, as it continues to monitor the issue.

item.title: Gold Coast average general rates up 4.7pc, debt to rise beyond $1b as budget adopted
item.description: Councillors have adopted the $2.7 billion budget, which is focused on major projects and frontline services.

item.title: World War II Lancaster bomber pilot Howard Hendrick dies aged 102
item.description: Howard Hendrick's life was dedicated to service in the Royal Australian Air Force and to his community. His contributions will forever remembered by his family and his hometown.

item.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince convicted of rape, sentenced to 4 years' jail
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, 29, has been found guilty of two counts of rape and other crimes.

item.title: Economists defend capital gains changes despite flaws as business groups line up in opposition
item.description: Economists have offered qualified support for the government's plans on day one of a Senate inquiry.

item.title: Four-week coronial inquest into Kumanjayi White's death in police custody set for April 2027
item.description: A four-week coronial inquest into the death of a 24-year-old man who died in a Coles supermarket in Alice Springs last year after being restrained by police officers has been set down for April 2027.

item.title: Man accused of lighting Mount Gambier Valley Lake bushfire has charge dismissed
item.description: A man who was charged after a fire near Mount Gambier will still have to appear in court to face harm, theft and offensive language charges.

item.title: Cairns man charged after allegedly naming prominent Queenslander in extortion case
item.description: A Cairns man accused of exposing the identity of a Queensland public figure at the centre of a mysterious extortion case has been charged by police with allegedly disobeying a suppression order.

item.title: Bold BBL plan within reach after Cricket Australia meets with states
item.description: A meeting between Cricket Australia and the six state bodies has ended in significant steps being taken towards BBL privatisation.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: As progress dips in men's netball, the Giants may have played their last game
item.description: Despite men's netballers making a valuable contribution to the Aussie Diamonds pathway and Super Netball competition, progress between the two genders at the international level has largely been scaled back and unexplained.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: The family behind Socceroo hero Nestory Irankunda
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: AFP inquiring into flotilla activist claims of sexual assault by Israeli defence personnel
item.description: Australian Federal Police have begun inquiries into allegations that Israeli defence personnel sexually assaulted Australian activists on a flotilla headed to Gaza, following a meeting with Foreign Minister Penny Wong.

item.title: Seven Australians are dying each day from drug overdose in worst year on record, researchers say
item.description: Australia recorded its deadliest year on record for drug overdoses, according to a new analysis, with an average of seven deaths per day.

item.title: New Zealand denied maiden win as Iranians protest outside stadium
item.description: Protesters demonstrate outside the stadium as Iran opens its World Cup with a 2-2 draw against New Zealand, ending a day of stalemates.

item.title: US and Iran sign agreement but key details remain unclear
item.description: Conflicting information is being released about the US-Iran ceasefire agreement announced at the weekend, including whether Iran will introduce tolls on the Strait of Hormuz in the future.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 3: Hoist the colours and try to answer these questions
item.description: Which country's flag is the only one with a modern weapon on it? And which US state has the Union Jack on its flag?

item.title: James Bond: 007 First Light is the secret agent's best outing in decades
item.description: While producers are still mulling over who will take the Bond mantle from Daniel Craig, the super-spy has already moved on in 007 First Light.

item.title: Cooling economy and Iran backdown to keep RBA at bay
item.description: Reserve Bank board members will be feeling relieved today when they meet to thrash out our future interest rate movements.

item.title: In a season of tight finishes, these are the AFL teams coming up clutch in the final moments
item.description: It's been a season of tight finishes in the AFL, but which teams are holding their nerve the best and why?

item.title: Transgrid seeks $1.1b from consumers in blowout 'litmus test' for energy transition
item.description: Transgrid wants to claw back huge cost increases for a major transmission line from consumers. Experts say the regulator's decision will have far-reaching consequences.

item.title: Saving might feel impossible, but there are five reasons to keep trying
item.description: Saving is not the only tool you need to achieve financial security these days, but it still has a role to play if you can manage it.

item.title: Tasmanian senator Jonno Duniam's resignation could open door for One Nation
item.description: Tasmanian senator Jonno Duniam's announcement that he intends to leave politics could be the leg up that One Nation needs.

item.title: Australian Crawl reforms for first concerts in 40 years
item.description: Australian Crawl will lead a major line-up of classic Australian acts across a run of shows later this year.

item.title: Keren Rowland's family suspects Ivan Milat killed her in 1971, as inquiry hears claims of lost evidence
item.description: A family says its private investigation suggests Keren Rowland's 1971 disappearance and death is linked to serial killer Ivan Milat as an inquiry hears claims of missing records, lost exhibits and unanswered questions.

item.title: Advocate says algal bloom information 'inconsistent', amid survey of health impacts of the bloom
item.description: Volunteers surveyed more than 200 people who reported a range of symptoms during the algal bloom outbreak not represented in the SA government's health advice. A disability advocate says health information has been "inaccessible" and "inconsistent".

item.title: The federal government says it fixed Australia's 'broken' environmental laws. Koala advocates disagree
item.description: The clearing of koala habitat at a Brisbane school has enraged advocates and raised questions about the federal government's sweeping changes to national environment laws.

item.title: EPA investigates fish kill after major fuel spill at Budgong on NSW South Coast
item.description: About 2,500 litres of fuel spilled into a creek on the NSW South Coast after a tanker became stuck on a dirt road 30 kilometres south of its scheduled delivery location.

item.title: Shark cull will not improve beach safety or reduce attacks, marine biologists say
item.description: Culling sharks would not reduce the risk of attacks, a marine biologist says, as the family of Sydney shark attack victim Leah Stewart says she has a "long road to recovery" ahead of her.

item.title: Premier promises $2 million for legacy project to honour Central Coast's Audrey Griffin
item.description: Chris Minns commits funding for a legacy project in memory of slain Central Coast woman Audrey Griffin.

item.title: PM confirms Treasury will release discussion paper for 'further input' on CGT changes — as it happened
item.description: Anthony Albanese says Treasury will release a discussion paper for "further input" on CGT changes, after Finance Minister Katy Gallagher reveals an extension to the fuel excise cut is under "active consideration".

item.title: Frankston beer microbiologist's orchid hobby is helping document rare species
item.description: Robert Mitchell's interest in rare orchids in the area around Frankston results in him naming three and penning a research paper.

item.title: Brendon McCullum worried about England captain Ben Stokes after nightclub incident
item.description: England coach Brendon McCullum says he went from "bewildered to angry to gutted" after hearing captain Ben Stokes was involved in another late-night incident.

item.title: Accused criminals flown out of remote communities as WA regional courts shut
item.description: WA Police have been forced to fly some accused criminals out of remote communities since the start of this month due to regional court closures sparked by a security dispute.

item.title: Australian World Cup referee Shaun Evans responds to accusations he used a hate symbol at World Cup
item.description: The Australian referee at the centre of a controversy around an alleged hate symbol being used during the World Cup broadcast has responded.

item.title: Recreational anglers demand end to tunnel-net trials in north Queensland
item.description: The nets are being floated as a replacement for the controversial gillnets, which will be banned from June next year.

item.title: Burrowing bettongs returned to outback decades after local extinction
item.description: Wiped off mainland Australia by the introduction of cats and foxes, burrowing bettongs are making a triumphant comeback in outback NSW.

item.title: Mount Gambier resident Ben Whitehead in remission after surprise cancer trial results
item.description: Ben Whitehead was given just months to live after being diagnosed with a rare cancer that doctors had not seen before. A second clinical trial has delivered hope.

item.title: Melville tree protection plans in doubt as council meeting weighs options
item.description: Plans to protect established trees on private property have had a setback in Perth's southern suburbs, with councillors concerned that protections may go too far.

item.title: When is State of Origin II? Kick-off time and how to watch Blues vs Maroons
item.description: Game II of the men's 2026 State of Origin series will be held this Wednesday at the Melbourne Cricket Ground. Here's what to know about the match.

item.title: White House UFC event not happening again, president Dana White says
item.description: Despite touting the massive success of the White House event, UFC boss Dana White says the company will never stage another event like it.

item.title: US-Iran agreement confirms Trump trend on the art of the deal
item.description: The ceasefire agreement fits a pattern from Donald Trump where the signing of a deal appears to be the major goal and the details often seem a distant secondary consideration.

item.title: World Cup: Cabo Verde fans celebrate shock draw with Spain with colour and pride
item.description: Cabo Verde fans revel in the biggest shock of this World Cup so far, the tournament debutants holding European champion Spain to a 0-0 draw.

item.title: Gavin Newsom tells Trump to leave his wife out of investigations
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Allied health workers walk off the job in Victoria as pay negotiations break down
item.description: The strike involves radiographers, sonographers, physiotherapists, podiatrists, speech pathologists and social workers at the state's public hospitals.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire deal shaped by China and Russia, experts say
item.description: Chinese, Russian and Iranian officials met about a day before the deal was publicly announced, with experts saying it proves Beijing and Moscow helped shape the agreement.

item.title: Pensioner veers into riders causing terrifying crash at Germany's LVM Saarland Trofeo
item.description: An elderly woman on a mobility scooter has caused a frightening crash during a cycling race in Germany after riding into the path of the competitors.

item.title: South Australia's Electoral Commissioner Mick Sherry resigns
item.description: South Australia's Electoral Commissioner Mick Sherry has resigned amid a review into the scandal-plagued state and Voice elections.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is expected to finally reopen within days. Then what?
item.description: The months-long shutdown of the the crucial shipping route has rocked global oil markets and flowed through to all sorts of prices. How will the reopening change things, and how fast?

item.title: Solomon Islands prime minister raises Pacific security pact proposal during Australia visit
item.description: Solomon Islands's new prime minister raises the idea of a Pacific-wide security treaty while meeting Prime Minister Anthony Albanese this month.

item.title: Artemis III pilot Luca Parmitano recalls witnessing 'devastating' Black Summer bushfires from space
item.description: Luca Parmitano was commanding the International Space Station when bushfires devastated eastern Australia. He has a blunt warning for the future of the planet.

item.title: How having older parents transformed my life at a young age
item.description: Having older parents has shaped my life in many ways, including taking on a carer role for Mum and Dad in my 20s.

item.title: Former ACT attorney-general Gordon Ramsay facing new charges after arrest for child grooming
item.description: Former ACT attorney-general Gordon Ramsay was to have been committed to trial in the ACT Supreme Court today, but instead his lawyers have asked for more time to prepare the case as he is facing seven additional charges.

item.title: Mark Waden found guilty of murdering American partner Priscilla Brooten
item.description: Mark Sheridan Waden has been found guilty of murdering Priscilla Brooten in 2018 and sentenced to life in prison with a 20-year non-parole period.

item.title: Police receive 'new information' about Brisbane doctor charged with almost 100 counts of rape
item.description: Former Brisbane GP Stellios Theodoros is facing 148 counts of rape and sexual assault relating to an alleged excessive number of pelvic examinations, pap smears and breast checks.

item.title: NSW Police investigate video of officer pushing woman at Wollongong Bisalloy protest
item.description: NSW Police will review footage showing a woman falling to the ground after being pushed by an officer during a pro-Palestine protest outside Bisalloy Steels in Wollongong.

item.title: Coffs Harbour toddler's death a 'tragedy' but not murder, court told
item.description: A court has heard a man accused of murdering a two-year-old boy would lock the toddler in a dark garage as a form of punishment.

item.title: On the last big day, Leichhardt Oval showed its power one more time
item.description: On the day of the final NRL game at Leichhardt Oval before the old ground changes forever, rugby league's broken-down palace reminded us all of its greatest powers.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Cabo Verde finds player on LinkedIn, meet Merlin the Duck
item.description: Cabo Verde's incredible result against Spain brings with it no shortage of amazing stories, while a duck in a jersey is stealing hearts in Mexico. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

item.title: Mother of murdered Broome man Josh Warneke slams WA Police culture and flawed investigation
item.description: Ingrid Bishop says poor culture within WA Police hampered the investigation into her son Josh Warneke's death from the start.

item.title: KPMG referred to anti-corruption watchdog amid three-month freeze on Commonwealth contracts
item.description: KPMG is facing a three-month freeze on new federal government contracts amid a worsening scandal over the treatment of a whistleblower, with the Greens now referring the beleaguered company to the national anti-corruption watchdog.

item.title: Paedophile teacher sentenced for historical sex offences at Traralgon school
item.description: The court hears William Gilfillan, more commonly known as Rob, will be 92 years old when he becomes eligible for parole, and he will likely die in jail.

item.title: Emergency care delayed before death of wellness influencer Stacey Warnecke, court hears
item.description: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

item.title: Retailer Lincraft to close remainder of shopfronts and move entirely online
item.description: Retailer Lincraft will close the remainder of its shopfronts after more than 80 years of providing fabric, materials and homewares to Aussie crafters.

item.title: World Cup: After complicated build-up, Iran finally plays amid mixed feelings from diaspora
item.description: After a challenging, politically charged build-up to their World Cup campaign Iran's 2-2 draw with New Zealand brought mixed feelings for the Iranian diaspora.

item.title: Reserve Bank keeps interest rate at 4.35pc as Australia's economy slows down
item.description: The Reserve Bank keeps the interest rate at 4.35pc as Australia's economy slows down.

item.title: Albury ratepayers asked to consider rate hike of up to 42pc over two years to fill financial hole
item.description: Albury residents could face one of the largest NSW council rate increases of recent years, with councillors voting to seek community feedback on plans to help address its estimated $18 million of debt.

item.title: Inquest into murder of Bridgette 'Biddy' Porter underway in NSW
item.description: Biddy Porter's killer was found not criminally responsible for her murder in NSW in 2020. After years of campaigning from her parents, a coronial inquest is examining the lead-up to Biddy's death.

item.title: Contempt charge considered for Cairns man who allegedly breached suppression order
item.description: A Cairns man who posted details of an extortion case to social media faces charges of breaching a non-publication order. A police prosecutor and a magistrate have warned him about serious legal implications that may come next.

item.title: Visitors react to algae in Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: El Niño declared by BOM and it could become the strongest on record
item.description: El Niño is a state that can linger for up to 12 months and disrupt weather patterns across the globe, with Australia especially prone.

item.title: Cameron Munster backs Maroons teammate Tino Fa'asuamaleaui to honour late dad in State of Origin II
item.description: Queensland captain Cameron Munster is confident prop Tino Fa'asuamaleaui will do his late father Fereti proud in tomorrow night's State of Origin II at the MCG.

item.title: Braden Jentian sentenced to life in prison for 'brutal' murder of young partner in Katherine
item.description: Braden Jentian, 38, has been sentenced to life behind bars for the "protracted and highly brutal murder" of his 22-year-old female partner, whose body was found in a vacant block in Katherine in 2024.

item.title: 'Global' midwife shortage temporarily shuts Crystal Brook and Jamestown birthing services in SA's Mid North
item.description: Two more South Australian regional centres are now temporarily diverting expectant mothers as Kapunda Hospital's birthing service is closed indefinitely.

item.title: Aftermath of Russian strike on UNESCO-listed Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra monastery
item.description: The historic Dormition Cathedral of the Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra suffered "extensive" damage in an attack Ukraine says was targeted at erasing its cultural heritage and identity.

item.title: Meet the Iranian women who support the regime and see Trump as the villain
item.description: As we journey into the heart of Iran, we meet the women who support the clerics and want revenge on the man who killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Terella Brewing fined following legal battle with Sunshine Coast Council
item.description: A once popular brewery has been fined in court after shutting up shop earlier this year due to an ongoing legal battle with a Queensland council.

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith cleared to attend Australian War Memorial event after judge varies bail conditions
item.description: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith's bail conditions have been varied so he can attend an opening at the Australian War Memorial in Canberra next week.

item.title: Blues coach Laurie Daley reveals Ethan Strange's role in State of Origin II against Maroons at MCG
item.description: After his starring role for the Blues in State of Origin I, Ethan Srange is given a new assignment against the Maroons at the MCG on Wednesday night.

item.title: Trio found guilty of murdering 14yo Ethan Hoac in St Albans machete attack
item.description: A third man has been found guilty of murdering 14-year-old Ethan Hoac in an unprovoked machete attack in Melbourne's north-west in 2023.

item.title: Jury hears man accused of wife's attempted murder told her 'I'm going to kill you' before stabbing
item.description: A jury hears a Canberra man on trial for allegedly attempting to murder his wife whispered "I'm going to kill you" in her ear shortly before stabbing her in the arm with a kitchen knife.

item.title: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor meets Kumanjayi Little Baby's grandmother in Alice Springs
item.description: Federal Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has visited Alice Springs, where he has met family of Kumanjayi Little Baby and renewed the Coalition's calls for deeper scrutiny of federal funding directed to the region.

item.title: 'Reasonable possibility' father injected insulin himself, murder trial jury told
item.description: A woman standing trial accused of murdering her parents with insulin has denied the allegations, while her defence lawyer has told the jury the possibility one of the alleged victims injected a fatal dose of insulin himself cannot be ruled out.

item.title: Tunisia names Hervé Renard as new FIFA World Cup coach after sudden axing of Sabri Lamouchi
item.description: Tunisia wastes no time in appointing a new coach at the World Cup following the sacking of Sabri Lamouchi in the wake of the humiliating 5-1 loss to Sweden.

item.title: Queensland teen allegedly planned attack on LNP over nuclear policy, court hears
item.description: A teenager accused of planning a terror attack in Brisbane allegedly wanted to target the Liberal National Party over their nuclear policy.

item.title: Alice Springs branch of Victims of Crime NT to close down down amid funding insecurity
item.description: The Alice Springs branch of Victims of Crime NT will be closed by the end of the week, with the organisation's chief executive citing funding insecurity. The closure will mean victims in the region will only be able to access services remotely.

item.title: With the RBA holding rates steady, the economic forecast is wait and see
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Officer tells inquest he gave Far North Qld man one second to drop knife before fatal shot
item.description: A Special Emergency Response Team officer tells a coronial inquest it took one second or less to storm a bathroom and fire the shot that killed Far North Queensland man Aubrey Donahue during a hostage siege.

item.title: Iran deal says 'loud and clear' that Tehran will not have a nuclear weapon: Trump
item.description: Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 in France, Donald Trump says Iran will never have nuclear weapons.

item.title: Russian artist who satirised Vladimir Putin shot dead in Poland
item.description: Two Belarusian nationals are arrested in connection with the killing, authorities say.

item.title: G7 leaders agree to pressure Russia to end war with Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump says Moscow should "make a deal" to end the war with Ukraine as G7 leaders have agreed to intensify pressure on Russia.

item.title: FBI says plot to attack White House UFC event with drones thwarted
item.description: US media says five people are in custody in connection with the plot, that involved flying explosive-laden drones towards buildings near last weekend's White House UFC match.

item.title: Russian frigate fires warning shots near UK waters to avert yacht collision
item.description: Britain and Russia have issued statements to confirm that a Russian frigate fired warning shots to divert a UK-flagged civilian yacht near British territorial waters on Tuesday.

item.title: Live: Messi equals World Cup record with hat-trick, as Jordan battles Austria
item.description: Lionel Messi scores three as Argentina beats Algeria, after Erling Haaland scored twice as Norway beat Iraq 4-1 in Boston and Kylian Mbappé's super strike sunk Senegal. Follow live.

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia, Marathon, Mixtape and more — the best video games from the first half of 2026
item.description: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

item.title: Ann Patchett rewrites the step-family narrative in her new novel, Whistler
item.description: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: State of Origin Game II
item.description: Today's ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to go deep into AFL territory and come away with the win. Mouthguards in, let's go.

item.title: Shadow Treasurer Tim Wilson holds undisclosed shares in climate advisory firm through family trust
item.description: The shadow treasurer's investment trust holds 15,000 shares in a small company called Gondwana Carbon which helps developers to acquire land, develop carbon abatement projects, earn carbon credits and then trade them.

item.title: Fuel shock expected to be felt for years as businesses urge extended relief
item.description: Small-town coffee shops and long-established businesses are at risk of closing despite a ceasefire in the Middle East easing the global oil price. Regional businesses say fuel relief measures need to be extended because the damage done from the fuel shock is far from over.

item.title: RBA governor Michele Bullock is still threatening rate hikes. The market thinks she's bluffing
item.description: Michele Bullock made it abundantly clear that the RBA was not ruling out further interest rate rises, but the market appears to be unswayed by the tough talk.

item.title: Marauding cockatoos thwarted by bin lid invented by men's shed
item.description: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

item.title: What time is State of Origin II kick-off? How to watch Blues vs Maroons at MCG
item.description: NSW and Queensland meet at the famous MCG in State of Origin Game II. Here is what you need to know ahead of tonight's match.

item.title: What is in Donald Trump's Iran deal and what does Tehran hope to gain?
item.description: Iran agreeing to a ceasefire deal with the United States tells us Tehran likely had something to gain from signing on. This is what Iran is promoting as the 14-point deal.

item.title: Visitors to Hobart's Kunanyi/Mount Wellington should pay, tourism consultancy recommends
item.description: A tourism consultancy firm has recommended that visitors to Hobart's Kunanyi/Mt Wellington be charged for access — likely to result in a "deliberate and positive outcome" of fewer people going.

item.title: Gout Gout clocks personal best in 150m stoush with Noah Lyles at Ostrava Golden Spike
item.description: Rising Australian superstar Gout Gout pushes American champion Noah Lyles all the way in their first race against each other.

item.title: Australian pensioners finding rent and cost-of-living relief in moving overseas
item.description: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

item.title: Queensland government signs new long-term lease for 'life changing' school for autistic children
item.description: The private Sycamore School in Alexandra Hills is so sought after, some parents drive almost 50 kilometres one-way to drop off their kids, but it was "on the chopping block".

item.title: NDIS participants highlight mental toll of navigating 'complex' system
item.description: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

item.title: Interest-free loans and cash subsidies available to NSW residents for renewable energy uptake
item.description: Interest-free loans and cash subsidies are being offered by the New South Wales government to encourage the uptake of renewable energy measures like solar panels and household batteries.

item.title: How the explosion of the ultra-wealthy risks democracy
item.description: Democracy cannot survive trillionaires. We need to start taxing billionaires properly.

item.title: Childcare workers scrap walk-off as government commits to pay rise extension
item.description: Early educators had threatened to walk off the job to keep a federally funded 15 per cent pay subsidy due to end in November.

item.title: Mystery of saltwater crocodile dumped on side of Adelaide road continues
item.description: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

item.title: Muswellbrook councillor manages shop that sells illegal tobacco
item.description: A councillor from the Upper Hunter in NSW manages a tobacco shop that sells illegal cigarettes as experts warn the black-market crisis is deepening in regional areas.

item.title: State of Origin coaches back referee Ashley Klein after gambling history made public
item.description: Billy Slater and Laurie Daley come to the defence of referee Ashley Klein after his gambling debt history was revealed earlier in the week.

item.title: Queensland sets 65 per cent recycling target as waste outpaces population growth
item.description: The Queensland government wants to more than double the household recycling rate from 28 per cent to 60 per cent.

item.title: Jeremy Rockliff wants to move forward after cabinet resignations — but will the parliament let him?
item.description: Tasmania's powerful crossbench forced Madeleine Ogilvie and Jane Howlett out the door. Jeremy Rockliff says it's now time to "move forward".

item.title: Live: Activist group claims credit for banner stunt at Pauline Hanson's Press Club address
item.description: GetUp! says it is was responsible for the banner that unfurled during Pauline Hanson's first-ever address to the National Press Club. Follow live.

item.title: Windorah's new solar farm and battery system expected to cut diesel usage by half
item.description: This outback town is giving renewable energy another shot as a new solar farm and battery system hooks up to the grid, two decades after a solar array project failed to live up to the hype.

item.title: Trump intensifies pressure on Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks
item.description: The US president levels more criticism at Israel's prime minister and suggests asking Syria to take on Hezbollah "if Israel can't do the job without killing everyone else".

item.title: Women forced to labour in RDH tearoom, run-down wards as NT government shuts down calls for new hospital
item.description: From a mother forced to labour in a hospital's tearoom, to a baby sent home with jaundice on a busy night, doctors warn they are no longer able to provide NT patients with the standard of care they deserve.

item.title: US-Iran agreement sparks fertiliser hope for farmers
item.description: Farmers are hoping the US-Iran deal will help free up fertiliser supply and lower costs during the winter cropping season.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands reaches settlement with ARN Media over termination of $100m contract
item.description: Veteran Sydney broadcaster Kyle Sandilands and ARN Media reach a settlement to end a $100 million contract dispute, which had been playing out in the Federal Court.

item.title: Lucky escape for Perth man allegedly caught with poisonous death cap mushrooms
item.description: A man dodges potentially fatal poisoning after allegedly mistaking lethal death cap mushrooms for their hallucinogenic counterparts while illegally foraging in southern Western Australia.

item.title: World Cup 2026: US star issues ominous warning to Socceroos ahead of clash
item.description: American midfielder Sebastian Berhalter sends a tough warning to the Socceroos after a physical encounter between the US and Australia in a friendly last year. The two teams meet again on Saturday morning.

item.title: Live: ASX up, oil plunges to three-month low on reports US to scale back Iran sanctions
item.description: Oil prices sink to a three-month low on reports the US will allow Iran to start selling oil again, while the Australian share market edges up after a sell-off across Wall Street's tech and AI stocks. Follow live.

item.title: Kylian Mbappé denied penalty after 'nonsense' decision from A-League referee Alireza Faghani
item.description: Kylian Mbappé scores two more World Cup goals to bring his overall tally to 14, just two shy of the all time record. But the biggest talking point comes when A-League referee Alireza Faghani denies the French superstar a penalty, with the call slammed by experts.

item.title: More support urged for men's suicide prevention as advocates call for greater focus on situational distress
item.description: There are urgent calls for more customised support services for men and boys experiencing situational distress.

item.title: Queensland government accused of removing Aboriginal people from health roles in 'Project Invisibility'
item.description: The Queensland government will not deny the existence of "Project Invisibility", a name a barrister says is used internally to refer to the deliberate removal of Aboriginal people from leadership roles.

item.title: Two men charged over alleged murder of Chris Baghsarian, six now before the courts
item.description: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged another two men following an investigation into the alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian earlier this year in Sydney's north.

item.title: Bluey translated into an Indigenous language for the first time
item.description: The animated award-winning cartoon series Bluey has been translated into Yolŋu Matha, from north-east Arnhem Land, for NAIDOC Week.

item.title: WA rail union pushes back on proposal for safety lights on trains
item.description: The state's union for train drivers says a draft bill mandating more lights on trains is "fundamentally flawed" and would not fix motorists' behaviour at level crossings.

item.title: Prince George heading to Eton College to continue education
item.description: Prince George, who turns 13 in July, will attend Eton College this September, following in the footsteps of his father, Prince William.

item.title: Premier Jeremy Rockliff confirms legal fees have been used by two former ministers to fight Integrity Commission
item.description: Jeremy Rockliff has confirmed in parliament that taxpayer-funded legal fees have been used by former ministers Jane Howlett and Madeleine Ogilvie to fight the Integrity Commission.

item.title: ASIC launches criminal investigation into Zone RV as new allegations emerge
item.description: The former director of Zone RV faces a criminal probe into allegations he acted recklessly or dishonestly before the manufacturer collapsed with debts of $42 million.

item.title: Star casino's former CEO and lawyer penalised after Chinese money laundering scandal
item.description: Two former executives of The Star Entertainment Group Ltd have been handed penalties for breaching the Corporations Act in connection with a Chinese money-laundering scandal.

item.title: Organised crime gangs who recruit children could face life in jail under new Victorian laws
item.description: The Victorian government is raising the maximum penalty for recruiting children to commit crimes as it seeks to shut down organised crime syndicates driving the state's crime wave.

item.title: Australia downgrades travel warnings for Middle East but region remains volatile
item.description: Australia has downgraded its Middle East travel advice for key Gulf hubs, easing warnings from "Do Not Travel" to "Reconsider your need to travel".

item.title: How police linked Sydney childcare worker David James to dark web posts
item.description: Investigators were knocking on the door of a disgraced Sydney childcare worker within a week of him posting images of children on the darknet after identifying the user through metadata and CCTV.

item.title: Runaway Gold Coast Tasmanian devil Mary recaptured after two-week search
item.description: A spokesperson for Village Roadshow Theme Parks said Mary, the two-year-old marsupial, was found and recaptured overnight.

item.title: The night Scotland took over Fenway Park and painted baseball in tartan
item.description: Throughout the famed but understated bleachers of Boston's Fenway Park, an army of Scottish football fans created a night few fans will ever forget.

item.title: Former Don Dale Youth Detention Centre inmates have payout for tear gas incident slashed by High Court
item.description: The High Court slashes an award for exemplary damages for four former inmates from Darwin's notorious Don Dale Youth Detention Centre who were affected by a tear gas incident in 2014.

item.title: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore flags job losses at Pilbara operations
item.description: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore has cut jobs a year after merging its two iron ore businesses into a single entity, but won't say how many.

item.title: G7 leaders could stand up to Donald Trump and swap flattery for frankness
item.description: In France, the US president finds himself among a group of emboldened counterparts, many of whom now seem more comfortable criticising him publicly than ever before.

item.title: Sarah Game leaves her own party to join conservative Family First party
item.description: SA upper house MP Sarah Game has abandoned the political party she founded to join the conservative Family First brand, marking the third time she has changed parties in less than two years.

item.title: Brisbane City Council brings in 3.97 per cent rate hike in 2026/2027 budget
item.description: Lord Mayor Adrian Schrinner says Brisbane is paying the cost of natural disasters such as Cyclone Alfred and a lack of funding from the state and federal governments.

item.title: Federal government announces more than half a billion dollars in sports funding ahead of Brisbane 2032 Games
item.description: The $513 million two-year package will include cost of living assistance for athletes, funding for dozens of sporting programs, and boosts for Winter and Paralympic athletes.

item.title: New SA laws would remove serious offenders' rights over victims' remains
item.description: The South Australian government will introduce legislation to remove the rights of perpetrators of serious crimes to decide what happens with the remains of their victims.

item.title: Glue Store closes after posting $8.4m loss as economic pain hits retailers
item.description: Glue Store announces its permanent closure, becoming the latest Australian retailer to fail this year.

item.title: Two-thirds of scam compounds 'bypassed' in Cambodia's crackdown, Amnesty finds
item.description: A report by Amnesty International casts doubt on the impact of Cambodia's high-profile scam industry crackdown, finding authorities failed to dismantle the "vast majority" of operations.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Kylian Mbappé's banger, Marcelo Bielsa's morose pose and a full kit baby Jesus
item.description: Kylian Mbappé's magical strike thrust him back into the World Cup spotlight, while master coach Marcelo Bielsa did everything he could to avoid it. French journalists leave an empty space and Jesus gets a Mexican kit makeover. Here's the five quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: CFMEU accused of 'dark strategy' to generate millions in 'illegitimate' revenue
item.description: An inquiry into alleged union misconduct and criminal links in Queensland hears the CFMEU weaponised workplace agreements to bolster its revenue.

item.title: Rockhampton Zoo raises newborn chimp on human colostrum after mum accidentally drops infant
item.description: Rockhampton Zoo is hand-rearing a chimp whose mother dropped it from a height by accident hours after giving birth.

item.title: JetSki mustering on Cowarie Station
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dams are full at Quondong Station in South Australia.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pastoralists take to the skies to track down livestock lost in floods
item.description: Closed roads and flooded plains are making livestock inaccessible for some pastoralists, while others are employing some serious technology to track down their animals.

item.title: Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, Erling Haaland delight as World Cup stars come out to play
item.description: Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick, while Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland both scored braces on a night where the World Cup's biggest stars shone brightest.

item.title: Church youth leader who sexually assaulted minor 'knew it was wrong', court hears
item.description: Joshua Emonson, 24, earlier pleaded guilty to sexually assaulting a 13-year-old girl near Sunbury train station in February.

item.title: Chimp baby
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Socceroos star Alessandro Circati reacts to USA warning
item.description: Socceroos defender Alessandro Circati offers a blunt reply to Sebastian Berhalter's comments ahead of the World Cup clash between the two sides.

item.title: Townsville City Council unveils 2026–27 budget with 7.2 per cent rate increase
item.description: Townsville City Council will increase all minimum rates by 7.2 per cent, blaming inflationary pressures and fuel price hikes.

item.title: Australia names Commonwealth Games netball squad for gold medal defence in Glasgow
item.description: The Diamonds include five players set to make their Commonwealth Games debut as they look to win their fifth gold medal in Glasgow.

item.title: Killer of Bridgette 'Biddy' Porter wanted to play serial killer game the day before murder, inquest hears
item.description: The mother of Biddy Porter's killer has told a coronial inquest she regrets not being able to get mental health support for her child before the murder.

item.title: Climate change adding uncertainty to El Niño impact, scientists say
item.description: El Niño is declared and scientists say the climate change is making its impacts harder to predict.

item.title: Kingston Fire Brigade delivered emergency vehicle with multiple defects
item.description: It's a $600,000 fire-fighting truck, the first of 15 to enter service in Tasmania, but a list of faults has sent it back for warranty repairs.

item.title: Melbourne woman Zeinab Ahmad, accused of owning a Yazidi slave, refused bail
item.description: A woman accused of slavery offences while supporting the Islamic State group in Syria has had her bail application refused.

item.title: Andrew Thaler faces court over allegedly driving into neighbour's car
item.description: Andrew Thaler is facing five charges related to a verbal argument with a neighbour in Nimmitabel.
